title: Ocean Cleanup Tracker Update — Apr 21, 2026
summary: The Ocean Cleanup has now removed 45 million kg of plastic from oceans and rivers — a tenfold increase since January 2024 — with 2025 the most productive year on record at 25 million kg. The IUCN formally upgraded the North Atlantic Right Whale to Critically Endangered this month with ~400 individuals remaining, while a U.S. federal court ruled the American lobster industry violates the Endangered Species Act. Global ocean protection crossed 10.01% for the first time, Ghana designated its first-ever Marine Protected Area, and UK researchers unveiled AI system BlueBiome to improve coral restoration success rates. The BBNJ High Seas Treaty, now in force since January 17, 2026, saw its third and final PrepCom session conclude with implementation progress but a critical gap on subsidiary body nominations that could delay the first high-seas MPAs.

title: Ocean Cleanup Tracker Update — Sat Apr 18, 2026
summary: IUCN Formally Upgrades North Atlantic Right Whale to 'Critically Endangered'. U.S.

title: Ocean Cleanup Tracker Update — Thu Apr 16, 2026
summary: The High Seas Alliance released an in-depth analysis on April 16, 2026 of the third and final BBNJ Preparatory Commission (PrepCom 3) session, which concluded April 2, 2026 at UN headquarters.

title: Ocean Cleanup Tracker Update — Tue Apr 14, 2026
summary: Ghana officially declared its first Marine Protected Area on April 14, 2026, following more than 15 years of advocacy by fishing communities, scientists, and NGOs. UK researchers unveiled BlueBiome on April 14, 2026 — an AI-driven system designed to assess coral health and detect bleaching thermal stress earlier than conventional monitoring.
